title: carlina acaulis subsp. simplex
intro: Carlina acaulis subsp. simplex, also known as the False Hawk's Beak or Musk Thistle, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) characterized by its distinctive, elongated flower spikes. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which have been utilized to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Carlina acaulis subsp. simplex include relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to support overall well-being and combat various ailments.
